summary: Sprint B1 — WhatsApp Business Cloud API → CRM. Created /app/backend/tests/test_whatsapp_sprint_b1.py with 15 pytest cases covering: (a) GET /api/whatsapp/webhook verify — plain-text challenge 200 on match, 403 on mismatch, auto-status→connected after success; (b) POST /api/whatsapp/webhook — covered indirectly via mock (direct Meta payload shape exercised by reading unit-level code path); (c) POST /api/whatsapp/mock-receive — JWT required, creates contact (fuente=whatsapp, etapa=nuevo, phone persisted), does NOT duplicate on repeat phone, workspace isolation (Pasta contact not visible to DigiActiva founder and vice versa); (d) POST /api/whatsapp/send — 502 with Meta 401 detail using fake DigiActiva creds, workspace.integrations.whatsapp.status→error + last_error persisted, 400 pending_credentials when creds empty (verified by creating a fresh empty workspace via /api/workspaces + /api/auth/switch-workspace); (e) GET /api/whatsapp/status — access_token_redacted shows only truncated form (no full token), all has_X fields are booleans, webhook_url built from BACKEND_PUBLIC_URL; (f) PUT /api/workspaces/{id}/integrations/whatsapp — auto-status webhook_ready/connected when all required fields filled, not_connected when verify_token cleared; (g) multi-tenant send — Pasta admin NEVER receives DigiActiva's EAA_fake token in errors, uses its own creds. Result: 15/15 PASS in 2.72s. Frontend Playwright verified: all 4 mock-tester testids (mock-phone/mock-text/mock-profile/mock-run), all 3 composer testids (whatsapp-composer/whatsapp-composer-input/whatsapp-composer-send), WHATSAPP channel chip in messages tab (2 mensajes: 'Hola, vi su web' + 'Mensaje dos'), last_error banner visible in Integraciones, contact detail drawer opens on click, WhatsApp composer only renders when contact has phone. Token security confirmed: no access_token string ever appears in /api/whatsapp/status or error responses or backend uvicorn logs.

minor.issue: Meta sends verify_token in query string (Meta spec) — ends up in uvicorn access logs. Unavoidable per Meta protocol, but consider redacting via custom middleware or lowering access log level for this path.

On parsing exception returns 200 with {status:'error', detail: str(e)}. Good for preventing Meta retries, but the exception string is echoed back to Meta and could leak internals in edge cases. Consider returning generic {status:'ok'} even on error (keep detail in logs only).

status written as 'connected' on 2xx Meta response — correct; but 'webhook_ready' is never auto-promoted to 'connected' until an actual Meta verify GET succeeds. Fine as-is, just noting: status model mixes 'verified webhook' and 'successful send' into the same 'connected' state.
No verification of X-Hub-Signature-256 against workspace.integrations.whatsapp.app_secret. Out of scope for B1 per statement, but recommended before accepting real Meta traffic.

ui_bugs.issue: Badge visual is uppercase via CSS (class 'uppercase') but DOM innerText is title-case ('Webhook listo', 'Conectado'). Playwright text-based matchers against 'WEBHOOK LISTO' fail unless case-insensitive; affects ONLY testability.

After successful mock-receive, Contactos list doesn't auto-refresh to surface the new WhatsApp contact — user must navigate away & back. Consider invalidating contacts cache or showing the created contact_id in the response toast with a link.
Still reads 'FOUNDER OS' even for workspace_admin. Should show workspace name.

action_items: (Optional) POST /api/whatsapp/webhook: when exception occurs, return only {status:'ok'} to Meta; keep detail in logs (avoid leaking internals).
(Optional/security) Implement X-Hub-Signature-256 verification using workspace.integrations.whatsapp.app_secret before processing inbound payloads.
(Optional/UX) After mock-receive success in Integraciones, invalidate contacts query or show 'Ver contacto' deep-link in the success message.
(Carried over) Sidebar 'FOUNDER OS' → show workspace name for workspace_admin.
(Optional) Consider access-log redaction for /api/whatsapp/webhook (verify_token appears in query string — inherent to Meta's GET verification).
critical_code_review_comments: whatsapp.py (~310 LOC) is clean and focused: single module for verify/receive/send/mock/status. Reuses messaging.record_message + upsert_contact_from_signal → excellent DRY with Sprint B0.

whatsapp.py: each request calls get_db() which instantiates a NEW AsyncIOMotorClient per request (same pattern as chat.py). Use a module-level client to avoid connection-pool churn. Carried over from iter_6/7.

whatsapp.py /send: status changes to 'connected' on a successful send but the integration status model also uses 'connected' exclusively for 'webhook verified'. Consider splitting into send_status vs webhook_status, or just re-read as 'last send ok'.

whatsapp.py _process_inbound does not use X-Hub-Signature verification → any actor that knows the workspace_id OR phone_number_id could forge inbound. Fine for pre-prod / mock, but must be closed before exposing webhook publicly.
workspaces.py update_integration: auto-status for WhatsApp is duplicated (once generic, once re-computed) — refactor into a single helper.
CRMPage.js continues to grow (2100+ LOC) with WhatsAppComposer + WhatsAppMockTester + StatusBadge inlined. Split into /components/integrations/* and /components/contact/* to keep <700 LOC per file.
whatsapp.py _redact: 6-char prefix + 4-char suffix still reveals >50% of a 12-char token; ok for display but consider 3/3.
No rate limiting on /api/whatsapp/send — a rogue workspace_admin could spam Meta and burn the access_token quota.
Response of /api/whatsapp/send exposes wa_message_id — good for tracking; make sure it's never indexed by any non-workspace-scoped query.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on verified UI elements (mock tester, composer, message channel chip, last_error banner)

test_credentials: founder@digiactiva.com / digiactiva2025; admin@pastaalvuelo.com / pastaalvuelo2025. DigiActiva has seeded fake WA creds (waba_id=123, phone_number_id=456, access_token=EAA_fake, verify_token=vt, app_secret=as). Pasta has its own fake set (p1/p2/EAAp/vtp/asp).
seed_data_creation: Tests create per-run unique contacts via mock-receive (phones derived from uuid4; profile_name='TEST_Pedro_B1' / 'TEST_Cliente_Pasta'). A temp empty-creds workspace (slug 'test_wa_empty_<hex>') is created by test_send_missing_creds_400 and left behind (elite plan, no creds). Safe to delete.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_whatsapp_sprint_b1.py -v. Tests load both /app/backend/.env and /app/frontend/.env via python-dotenv — REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL is required. /api/auth/login returns {token, user}; founder-admin can use /api/auth/switch-workspace to change active ws (test relies on this for missing-creds path). /api/crm/messages/{cid} returns {messages: [], total: N} (NOT a plain list) — reuse this pattern. All 15 tests idempotent and ~3s runtime. Verify_token 'vt' is set for DigiActiva via seeded integrations and is required for webhook-verify tests. Status flow observed during this run: DigiActiva WA transitioned connected → error (post-send) → webhook_ready (post-PUT) — tests explicitly re-PUT at end to leave creds intact.
rca of the issue: No blocking issues. Three initial test failures on first run were all test-authoring bugs: (1) REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL lives in /app/frontend/.env — fixed by load_dotenv('/app/frontend/.env') in the test module; (2) /api/crm/messages/{contact_id} response is {messages, total} (dict), not list — fixed by reading payload['messages']; (3) test assumed Pasta workspace had empty WA creds, but Pasta already has fake creds from manual testing — replaced with a fresh empty workspace created+switched via /api/auth/switch-workspace. After fixes: 15/15 PASS.
